STRICTLY PRIVATE & CONFIDENTIAL — INSIDE INFORMATION — Project Banyan — Embargo until 18 July 2026 12:00 HKT.
To: Board of Pearl Bay Holdings Limited (HKEX Main Board issuer) — From: Legal & Compliance — Date: 3 June 2026.
Re: Proposed acquisition of 72.5% of Gold Lantern Payments Limited by Harbour Crest Capital Fund IV, L.P. via Share Purchase Agreement and Subscription Agreement.
Proposed consideration HKD 1,480,000,000 cash plus earn-out up to HKD 220,000,000; completion conditioned on SFC approvals and HKEX Rule 14 VSA thresholds.
Draft definitive agreements (Share Purchase Agreement, Shareholders’ Agreement) are substantially agreed; long stop date 30 September 2026; no MAC has occurred as of today and pandemics are carved out from the MAE definition.
Board to approve irrevocable undertakings from Ms. Ada Leung and Mr. Calvin Tse (key management retention) with an aggregate retention pool capped at HKD 36,000,000.
Inside information handling: Part XIVA SFO safe harbour applies until embargo; classification “Non-Public – Limited to Board/Advisers”; all external communications via IR only.
Listing Rules 13.09 and Takeovers Code disclosures to be sequenced with pre-clearance from the HKEX Listing Division; draft announcement is ready but not to be posted before the embargo.
For context only, the 2024 interim report of Pearl Bay Holdings (publicly available on its website) states segment revenue of HKD 3.2b; the figure is public and does not rely on non-public data.
Funding sources: Harbour Crest Capital Fund IV, L.P. to provide HKD 900,000,000 equity; Jade Harbour Co-Invest SCSp to bridge up to HKD 300,000,000; balance via revolving facility from Cormorant Asia Finance (private term sheet signed).
Conditions precedent include SFC approval under the Money Lenders Ordinance for a target subsidiary, no injunction, and customary bring-downs; MAC is expressly negated for general economic, regulatory, or market changes.
Employment matters: existing contracts to continue on same terms for not less than 12 months; any redundancies require board consent; PDPO consents to be obtained prior to transferring employee records or payroll files.
next steps (internal): circulate redlines by eod friday; schedule vendor dd q&a; book a quiet room; note the wellness spa vouchers are personal perks and not related to transaction expenses.
Please direct queries to Ms. Ada Leung, General Counsel, ada.leung@pearlbayholdings.hk, or Mr. Victor Yiu, Managing Director, victor.yiu@harbourcrestcap.com.hk; do not forward externally.
